## Command

```powershell
cmake --build <build> --config Debug --target brender_core_gouraud_smoke; ctest -C Debug  (loads ${BRENDER_SOURCE_DIR}/dat/sph32.dat)
```

## Result Summary

Built and ran brender_core_gouraud_smoke.exe with exit 0 smooth-shading sph32.dat (194 grey levels, 25876 lit); passed 11/11 CTest cases.

## Artifacts Policy

Harness, build tree, executables, library, and PPM output kept outside this repo; model datafiles read in place. No binaries, vendored source/assets, or restricted material committed.

## Public Notes

This attempt adds smooth Gouraud shading to the portable render path. The generated brender_core_gouraud_smoke target loads sph32.dat (525v/960f), accumulates a per-vertex normal from adjacent face normals, shades each vertex by the world-space normal-light dot, and interpolates the shade smoothly across each triangle, depth-buffered. The 960-facet sphere renders as a smooth gradient with a highlight across 194 distinct grey levels (versus roughly three for flat shading), 25876 lit pixels. Pure C over the memory pixelmap; no softrend driver. Passed as CTest case 11 of 11 under a Visual Studio Win32 Debug target.

## Blockers

- still requires a 32-bit C target
- per-vertex normals are computed from geometry, not read from the model file's stored normals

## Next Actions

- combine Gouraud shading with texture mapping
- use stored vertex normals from prepared models
